commit.message:
feat(engagement): quick capture service — inspiration decay + archive (ENG-04) (#145)

* feat(engagement): quick capture service — inspiration decay + archive (ENG-04)

Implement quickCaptureService.ts for Loss Aversion mechanism (Engagement
Engine §机制11). Factory pattern with:

- capture(): create inspiration entities in KG
- listUnused(): query unarchived, unused inspirations sorted by decay
- markUsed(): link inspiration to chapter (idempotent)
- archiveStale(): auto-archive > 14-day inspirations
- getDecayStats(): tier counts (fresh/reminder/fading/archived)
- dispose(): cleanup

Follows exact patterns of foreshadowingTracker.ts / storyStatusService.ts:
  DbLikeWithRun interface, prepared statements, 30s TTL cache,
  injectable clock, assertNotDisposed guard, parameter validation.

49 tests with in-memory SQLite covering: CRUD, decay tiers, boundary
conditions, cache invalidation, disposal, parameter validation, mixed states.

Closes #144

* fix(eng-04): address R1 audit findings — extend KG type system, fix comments, add boundary tests

- Add 'inspiration' and 'foreshadowing' to KNOWLEDGE_ENTITY_TYPES
- Create migration 002 to extend kg_entities CHECK constraint
- Fix getDecayStats comment-code mismatch (used items excluded from total)
- Add archiveStale 14-day boundary tests
- Extract shared DbRunStatement/DbLikeWithRun types to dbTypes.ts
- Update test schema to include CHECK constraint (now matches production)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(eng-04): R2 audit fixes — migration FK safety, tier classification, markUsed guard

R2 Audit Findings Addressed:
- [Duck #1] Migration 002: save/restore kg_relations around DROP TABLE
  to prevent CASCADE data loss with PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON
- [Duck #2] Add migration integration test with FK enforcement
  (projects + kg_entities + kg_relations, verify relations survive)
- [Duck #3] markUsed() now guards against archived items — SQL adds
  AND json_extract(attributes_json, '$.archived') IS NOT 1
- [Duck #4] classifyDecayTier returns 'archived' for >=14d per spec;
  getDecayStats switch handles 'archived' tier for unswept items
- [Duck #5] @deviation note on KNOWLEDGE_ENTITY_TYPES documenting that
  inspiration/foreshadowing use structured JSON incompatible with
  kgCoreService's Record<string, string> attribute model
- [Opus/Sonnet] foreshadowingTracker.ts @risk → @note referencing
  migration 002 (consistent with quickCaptureService.ts pattern)

Tests: 54 passing (was 51, +3 new: migration FK, markUsed archived, >=14d tier)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- storyStatusService.ts: @risk → @note at lines 135, 199 (migration 002
  resolved the foreshadowing type constraint)
- quickCaptureService.test.ts: migration FK test now uses exact production
  kg_relations column names (source_entity_id, target_entity_id,
  relation_type, description, created_at) matching 001_initial_schema.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(eng-04): R4 — listUnused 14d cutoff + test count comment

- SQL_LIST_UNUSED: add 'AND created_at > ?' to exclude >=14d items per spec
  (engagement-engine.md: '14天阈值：自动归档（不再首页提示）')
- listUnused(): compute 14d cutoff ISO timestamp and pass as 2nd param
- Update 3 tests (14d, 120d, 15d-unswept) to verify items NOT in listUnused
  but still correctly classified as 'archived' in getDecayStats
- Fix stale test count header: 51 → 54, add 3 R2-era test descriptions

Addresses: R3 Duck #3 (listUnused >14d items), R3 Opus #1 (stale count)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(kg,capture): R5 — type guard for dedicated-service entities + cache boundary post-filter

Duck R4 findings:
1. kgMutationSkill now rejects entity:create for 'inspiration'/'foreshadowing'
   types with INVALID_ARGUMENT — these must go through their dedicated services
   to avoid parseAttributes() mangling structured JSON attributes.
2. listUnused() cache now post-filters items that cross the 14d threshold
   during the 30s TTL window, preventing stale near-boundary items from
   appearing in the dashboard.

Tests: +3 (55 quickCapture + 22 kgMutationSkill = 2896 total)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(kg): R6 — guard entity:update patch.type for dedicated-service types

Duck R5 finding: entity:update payload carries patch.type (optional), which
allows converting a normal entity INTO inspiration/foreshadowing via generic
KG CRUD, reopening the parseAttributes() data corruption path.

Fix: kgMutationSkill validateMutation() now checks patch.type on entity:update
and rejects DEDICATED_SERVICE_ONLY_TYPES with INVALID_ARGUMENT.

Tests: +3 (inspiration/foreshadowing rejected, character allowed) → 2899 total

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(kgMutationSkill): runtime guard — reject entity:update on existing dedicated entities

Duck R6 found that even with patch.type blocked, a caller could still
entity:update an already-existing inspiration/foreshadowing entity by id,
overwriting attributes_json with Record<string,string> and corrupting
structured JSON (arrays/booleans).

Fix: execute() now calls entityRead() before entityUpdate() — if the
existing entity's type is in DEDICATED_SERVICE_ONLY_TYPES, returns
INVALID_ARGUMENT. 3 new tests cover inspiration, foreshadowing, and
the character-allowed positive case.

Tests: 154 files, 2902 tests, typecheck green.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(kgMutationSkill): fail-closed — propagate entityRead errors before entityUpdate

Duck R7 found that the runtime guard fails open: if entityRead() returns
DB_ERROR or NOT_FOUND, the guard was bypassed and entityUpdate() still
executed, reopening the corruption path for dedicated entities.

entityUpdate if entityRead succeeded AND type is not dedicated.

2 new tests: DB_ERROR propagated, NOT_FOUND propagated — both assert
entityUpdate is NOT called.

Tests: 154 files, 2904 tests, typecheck green.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
DOC-371: Add traffic policy structure reference (#2048)

Users had no clear, single place to understand the overall shape of a
traffic policy document — which top-level keys exist, how rules nest
within phases, and how expressions and actions compose together.

## Changes

- **`traffic-policy/structure.mdx`** — Created a new standalone
documentation page providing a comprehensive YAML structure reference:
- Full annotated template covering all three phases (`on_tcp_connect`,
`on_http_request`, `on_http_response`) with inline comments explaining
each field
- Rule fields reference table documenting `name`, `expressions`, and
`actions` (required vs. optional)
- Complete multi-rule, multi-phase example demonstrating real-world
patterns in both YAML and JSON formats

- **`docs.json`** — Added the new structure page to the documentation
navigation under "Getting Started" (before the agent/cloud endpoint
guides) for easy discoverability

The new structure page addresses user feedback requesting a clear
reference similar to AWS CloudFormation, Terraform, and NGINX
documentation that shows the complete document structure at a glance.

```yaml
on_http_request:
  # Rule 1: block a specific IP address
  - name: "block bad actor"
    expressions:
      - "conn.client_ip == '1.2.3.4'"
    actions:
      - type: deny
        config:
          status_code: 403

  # Rule 2: require authentication on /admin paths
  - name: "admin oauth"
    expressions:
      - "req.url.path.startsWith('/admin')"
    actions:
      - type: oauth
        config:
          provider: google

  # Rule 3: no expressions — forward all remaining traffic
  - actions:
      - type: forward-internal
        config:
          url: https://example.internal

on_http_response:
  - actions:
      - type: add-headers
        config:
          headers:
            x-frame-options: "DENY"
```

* fix: grab id_token before sign-out and stash in properties

The OIDC handler reads id_token from sign-out properties BEFORE
the event fires. Grab it in /logout while the auth cookie is still
valid and stash via Items[.Token.id_token]. Also fix SameSite

* fix: always send client_id in logout to prevent Keycloak rejection

Keycloak requires either id_token_hint or client_id when
post_logout_redirect_uri is used. The id_token doesn't reliably
survive re-login cycles in Blazor Server, so always send client_id
as a guaranteed fallback. Also always set post_logout_redirect_uri
regardless of tenant cookie state.

commit.message:
fix: cooperative ElvUI scale — write into E.db, let ElvUI apply it

Previously: we set UIParent:SetScale() → ElvUI overwrote it → hook fought
back 50ms later. Fragile, timing-dependent, E.mult often wrong.

New approach: write target into E.db.general.uiScale + E.uiScale, then call
E:UpdateUIScale(). ElvUI applies the scale itself and recalculates E.mult
in the same call — no fighting, no delays, E.mult always correct.

ApplyScale():
- Cooperative path (ElvUI present + hookElvUI enabled):
  set E.db.general.uiScale = target → E:UpdateUIScale()
  safety net: if ElvUI clamped the value, fallback to SetScale + manual mult
- Direct path (no ElvUI): UIParent:SetScale() as before

HookElvUI():
- Removed C_Timer.After(0.05) fighting pattern
- Hook now: if something external triggers UpdateUIScale with wrong scale,
  correct E.db.general.uiScale and call ApplyScale() cooperatively
- _applying guard prevents re-entrant loops

SyncElvUI():
- Simplified to just call ApplyScale() — cooperative path handles E.mult
- Manual repair button only; not needed in normal operation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(glowLayer): fix readiness check and spurious recompilation for NodeMaterial glow (#18297)

**Note**: this is an attempt to fix the flaky
"dissolve-effect-with-node-material-and-glow-layer" visualization test.

## Description

Fixes two bugs in the effect/glow layer when used with NodeMaterial and
`referenceMeshToUseItsOwnMaterial`:

### 1. Missing `_glowModeEnabled` during readiness check
(`thinEffectLayer.ts`)

When the effect layer checks readiness for meshes using their own
material, it calls `material.isReadyForSubMesh()` without setting
`_glowModeEnabled = true`. However, the actual render path in
`_renderSubMesh` **does** set it before calling
`renderingMesh.render()`.

This mismatch means the readiness check may compile the shader with
different defines than the render path expects (e.g. the
`ADDITIONAL_COLOR` define for NodeMaterial).

The fix sets `_glowModeEnabled` during the readiness check to mirror the
render path.

### 2. Spurious `markAsUnprocessed()` in
`FragmentOutputBlock.prepareDefines`

When the `additionalColor` (glow) input is **not** connected on a
`FragmentOutputBlock`, `_additionalColorDefineName` is never assigned
(remains `undefined`). The old code called `defines.setValue(undefined,
...)`, which sets a stray `defines["undefined"]` property. On the first
call, the value changes from JS `undefined` to `null`, triggering
`markAsUnprocessed()` and forcing an unnecessary shader recompilation
cycle.

The fix guards the call with `if (this._additionalColorDefineName)`.

## Testing

Tested with the `dissolve-effect-with-node-material-and-glow-layer`
visualization test (playground `#F6PGMC#12`).

commit.message:
Multi-target net8.0 and net9.0 for broader runtime compatibility (#93)

* Multi-target net8.0 and net9.0 for broader runtime compatibility

- Change TargetFramework to TargetFrameworks net8.0;net9.0 in AlRunner.csproj
  and AlRunner.Tests.csproj
- Use conditional package references for System.Diagnostics.EventLog and
  System.Configuration.ConfigurationManager (8.0.x for net8.0, 9.0.x for net9.0)
- Update CI workflows to install both .NET 8 and .NET 9 SDKs via setup-dotnet
  array syntax and run tests for each framework separately
- NuGet package will now include tools/net8.0/ and tools/net9.0/ so dotnet tool
  install automatically selects the matching runtime

Closes #75

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix CopyBCDlls target for multi-targeting

When switching from TargetFramework (single) to TargetFrameworks (multi),
MSBuild introduces an outer build where $(TargetFramework) is empty. The
CopyBCDlls target was evaluating _AlRunnerOut with an empty TFM segment,
producing paths like 'AlRunner/bin/Debug//dll' that don't exist.

Fix: add Condition="'$(TargetFramework)' != ''" so CopyBCDlls only runs
in inner builds (where $(TargetFramework) is set to net8.0 or net9.0).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix parallel download conflict and ServerTests framework ambiguity

Two issues introduced by multi-targeting:

1. EnsureALCompiler and EnsureBCServiceTierDlls targets in AlRunner.csproj
   now run for each TFM inner build. MSBuild runs inner builds in parallel,
   so net8.0 and net9.0 builds both try to download the same DLLs to the
   same shared cache path simultaneously, causing IOException (file in use).
   Fix: add 'TargetFramework == net8.0' condition so downloads only run
   once, in the net8.0 inner build. The net9.0 inner build finds the
   files already present.

2. CliServer.StartAsync() calls 'dotnet run --no-build --project AlRunner'
   which is ambiguous on a multi-targeted project — dotnet run requires
   --framework when TargetFrameworks (plural) is set. The server exits
   immediately, causing 'Server process exited before signaling readiness'.
   Fix: derive the current TFM from Environment.Version and pass
   --framework to dotnet run, matching the framework under which the
   test is running.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix download targets to run in outer build for multi-targeting

With <TargetFrameworks> (plural), MSBuild dispatches inner builds in
parallel. The previous fix (net8.0-only condition) caused net9.0's
ResolveAssemblyReferences to run before net8.0's download completed,
resulting in 'assembly not found' errors.

Correct fix: run EnsureALCompiler and EnsureBCServiceTierDlls in the
OUTER build via BeforeTargets=DispatchToInnerBuilds. This ensures DLLs
are fully present before either inner build starts.

Condition: 'TargetFrameworks == empty (single-target) OR TargetFramework
== empty (outer build)'. In inner builds, both TargetFrameworks and
TargetFramework are set, so the condition is false and no download is
attempted. In single-target builds, TargetFrameworks is empty so the
target runs before ResolveAssemblyReferences as before.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix CliRunner to pass --framework for multi-targeted project

dotnet run on a multi-targeted project requires --framework or it fails
immediately with exit code 1 and no output. IntegrationTests use CliRunner
which was missing --framework, causing all integration tests to fail.

Derive the TFM from Environment.Version (same as CliServer fix) to always
match the framework under which the test process is running.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

commit.message:
feat(ux-8): Split violations into [CONFIG] vs [ARCH] categories

CONFIG: Quick 10-minute fixes (add frontmatter, update descriptions, etc)
ARCH: Structural improvements requiring migration (real token savings)

Example output:

  [CONFIG] Quick fixes — 10 minutes each:
    skills/pdf/SKILL.md | Description is 6 chars

  [ARCH] Structural improvements (real token savings):
    skills/pdf/SKILL.md | Ungated skill eligible for migration

This prevents alert fatigue by helping users distinguish between
trivial fixes and impactful architectural changes.

commit.message:
Fix editor preview freezing on last 3+ seconds of playback

The preview compositor used the source video's FPS (e.g. 60) for frame
indexing while the compositor internally used a capped preview FPS (30).
This caused ComposeFrame to throw ArgumentOutOfRangeException for any
frame index beyond TotalFrames, which was silently caught, leaving the
preview frozen on the last successfully rendered frame.

Three fixes applied:

1. PreviewRenderer.RenderPreviewFrame now accepts TimeSpan position
   instead of a raw frame index, computing the correct compositor
   frame index internally using the preview output FPS with clamping.

2. EditorPage.UpdatePreviewFrameAsync defers _lastRenderedFrameIndex
   update until after a successful render, preventing transient failures
   from poisoning the skip-cache and blocking retries.

3. VideoWriter.ActualDuration now includes the last frame's display
   time (was lastTimestamp - firstTimestamp, missing one frame interval),
   fixing a fencepost error that could cause TotalFrames to be short.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
